title: OEIS A34693: the bound 1 + n^0.74 is not always valid (witness n=19)
informal_statement: OEIS A34693: a(n) is the smallest k such that n*k+1 is prime. This packet shows the candidate upper bound 1 + n^0.74 is NOT always valid for a(n): there exists n > 0 (witness n=19) such that for every k < 1 + n^0.74, n*k+1 is not prime -- i.e. a(19) >= 1 + 19^0.74, so a(19)/(1+19^0.74) is not <= 1.
informal_proof_idea: Witness n=19. Show 19^0.74 <= 9 by raising both sides to the 100th power (19^74 <= 9^100, a literal numeral comparison closed by norm_num) and using that x -> x^100 is injective/monotone on nonnegative reals. This bounds k < 1 + 19^0.74 <= 10, so k in {0,...,9}; check by exhaustive case split (interval_cases + norm_num) that 19k+1 is composite for each of these 10 values.
formal_statement_pp: theorem oeis_a34693_exists_k_best_possible : ∃ n > (0 : ℕ), ∀ (k : ℕ), (k:ℝ) < 1 + (n:ℝ) ^ ((74:ℝ)/100) → ¬(n * k + 1).Prime

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: ANTI-OVERCLAIM: this is a RESTATEMENT of the upstream exists_k_best_possible using Real.rpow ((n:ℝ) ^ ((74:ℝ)/100)) instead of the upstream Real.nthRoot 100 n ^ 74 -- mathematically identical for n >= 0 (nthRoot 100 n ^ 74 = n^(74/100)), NOT a mathematical simplification. The substitution was forced by an environment/API mismatch: `Real.nthRoot` does not resolve under this repo's pinned Mathlib revision (mathlib_search_declarations shows THIS environment's `nthRoot` is a different, Nat-valued function at Mathlib/Analysis/SpecialFunctions/Pow/NthRootLemmas.lean, not the Real-valued one the upstream file expects) -- a genuine cross-Mathlib-revision drift, not a proof bug. First attempt (verbatim transport of the upstream proof term) failed with kernel_fail 'Unknown constant Real.nthRoot'; this packet's proof was independently constructed via Real.rpow_natCast/Real.rpow_mul/pow_le_pow_iff_left₀ to reach the same numeric bound (19^0.74 <= 9, hence k < 10) using a different but equivalent route. Second attempt also needed a whitespace/indentation repair (raw_lean_block requires sibling tactics at the SAME column; an accidental 2-space over-indent on the `have hb` line after `refine` caused it to parse as nested rather than sibling, producing a parse error and leaving the root goal unsolved) -- fixed by aligning all top-level tactics to column 0, only indenting nested `by`-block bodies. Second attempt (episode 406c3860, step 2) reached kernel_verified. This is a genuinely solved OEIS/formal-conjectures literature companion result, not an open problem -- the file's actual open conjectures (exists_k, exists_k_stronger, a_isBigO, a_unbounded) are untouched and remain sorry upstream. training.eligibility: quarantined per this session's conservative default for adapted/restated frontier packets pending real external review. General lesson recorded in packets/frontier/formal_conjectures/BLOCKERS.md: upstream formal-conjectures files may reference API names that don't resolve under this repo's pinned Mathlib revision even when problem_imports includes "Mathlib" -- check via mathlib_search_declarations/lean_declaration_lookup before assuming a verbatim transport will compile, and be prepared to re-derive the same numeric fact via an equivalent but differently-named API.
